zh.lifeSummary: Helto Marmion de Bois d'Elle是Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth和Hawise的兒子，出生在法國諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯豐特納勒-馬米。他和Idonea FitzWilliam至少有2個兒子。他去世於1180年之前在英國爾切斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Helto Marmion de Bois d'Elle是Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth和Hawise的儿子，出生在法国諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯豐特納勒-馬米。他和Idonea FitzWilliam至少有2个儿子。他去世于1180年之前在英国爾切斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Marmion
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: perhaps an Anglicized form of an unidentified Gaelic name. Compare   Merriman  .
English (Lancashire; of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Old French marmion ‘monkey, brat’.

variants.name: Merriman
meanings: English:

nickname from Middle English mirie, merie, murie ‘merry, pleasant’ + man ‘man’. Compare Merry 1. This surname is established in Ireland (perhaps as an Anglicized form of an unidentified Gaelic name; compare Marmion ) as well as England.

there has probably been some confusion with Marriman, probably an occupational name meaning ‘servant of Mary’, from the female personal name Mary (see Marie ) + Middle English man in the sense ‘servant’, or perhaps a pet form of Mary using the hypocoristic suffix -man. The surname Marriman is now vanishingly rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Marmon
meanings: English (Lancashire and Gloucestershire):
variant of   Marmion  .
variant of Marriman, an occupational name meaning ‘servant of Mary’, from the female personal name Mary (see Marie ) + Middle English man in the sense ‘servant’.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bois
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

variants.name: Dubois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Pierre, Fernand, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Michel, Alain, Luc.

French, Walloon, and English (of French Huguenot origin): topographic name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ), or a habitational name for someone from any of several places in various parts of France and in Belgium (Wallonia) named Le Bois. This is the most frequent surname in Wallonia. It is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, and Haiti. In both England and North America it is sometimes translated into English as Wood . Compare Deboe , Debois , Deboy , Duboise , and Dubose .

History: This surname is listed (in the form Du Bois) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. One of the several Huguenot ancestors bearing this name was Chrétien Du Bois from Flanders, whose son, Louis Du Bois (born 1626 in Lille, France, died 1696 in Kingston, NY), was among the Huguenots who founded the town of New Paltz, NY 1675–78.

variants.name: Boisvert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Andre, Donat, Lucien, Jacques, Gaston, Normand, Pierre, Gilles, Herve, Adrien.
French:
topographic name for someone who lived in a dense forest or perhaps a copse of evergreens, from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + vert, verd ‘green’.

variants.name: Boisclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Marcel, Aldege, Alphonse, Arsene, Edmour, Emile, Leopaul, Prudent, Rejean, Remi, Yves.

French: topographic name from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + clair ‘clear, light’, or a habitational name from (Le) Bois Clair, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is virtually non-existing in France.

variants.name: Boies
meanings: Altered form of French   Bois  .

variants.name: Baskerville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Boscherville in Eure, France, named with Old Anglo-Norman French boschet ‘copse, thicket’ (a diminutive of Bois ) + ville ‘settlement, town’.

variants.name: Debois
meanings: Altered form of French   Dubois  , a cognate of 1 above.
French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ).

nameMeanings.name: d'Elle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: habitational name from a place so named in North Rhine-Westphalia or a topographic name (see   Dell  ).

French (Lorraine): habitational name from Delle, a place in Territoire de Belfort. Alternatively, a topographic name, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’, for someone who lived near a prominent alder tree or trees, from elle, a derivative of Middle High German erle ‘alder’.

variants.name: Dell
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived in a small, deep valley, from Middle English dell ‘dell’ (Old English dell ‘pit, valley’), or a habitational name arising from any of several minor places called Dell, derived from this word, for example, in Buckinghamshire, Essex, and Sussex.
English and Scottish: variant of   Dill  .
German: topographic name from Low German delle ‘dell, depression’ (Middle High German telle ‘gorge’).

variants.name: Del
meanings: French (Lorraine): variant of the habitational name Delle 2 or a masculine form of a homonymous topographic name. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Spanish or Italian preposition with fused definite article del ‘of the’, which forms first part of surnames such as Del Cid and Del Signore , has apparently been in some instances counted as an independent surname.

nameMeanings.name: Helto
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Helton in Askham (Westmorland), from Old English helde ‘slope’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Helto Marmion de Bois d'Elle was born in Fontenay-le-Marmion, Calvados, Normandy, France as the son of Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth and Hawise. He had at least 2 sons with Idonea FitzWilliam. He died before 1180, in Chester, Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Helto Marmion de Bois d'Elle est né à Fontenay-le-Marmion, Calvados, Normandie, France, fils de Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth et Hawise. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Idonea FitzWilliam. Il est décédé avant 1180, à Chester, Cheshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Helto Marmion de Bois d'Elle wurde in Fontenay-le-Marmion, Calvados, Normandie, Frankreich, als Sohn von Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth und Hawise geboren. Er hatte mit Idonea FitzWilliam mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb vor 1180 in Chester, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Helto Marmion de Bois d'Elle è nato il nel Fontenay-le-Marmion, Calvados, Normandie, Francia come figlio di Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth e Hawise. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Idonea FitzWilliam. Morì prima del 1180, nel Chester, Cheshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Helto Marmion de Bois d'Elleはフランスノーマンディカルヴァドスフォンテナイ-レ-マルミオンでRobert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and TamworthとHawiseの息子として生まれた。彼はIdonea FitzWilliamとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼は1180年以前にイギリスチェシュアイアチェスターで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Helto Marmion de Bois d'Elle(은)는 프랑스 노먼디 캘바도스 폰텐아이-레-마미언에서 Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth(와)과 Hawise의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Idonea FitzWilliam(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1180년 이전 영국 체실르 체스터에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Helto Marmion de Bois d'Elle nasceu em Fontenay-le-Marmion, Calvados, Normandie, França filho de Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth e Hawise. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Idonea FitzWilliam. Ele faleceu antes 1180, em Chester, Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Helto Marmion de Bois d'Elle родился в местности фонтенеи-ле-мармион, калвадос, норманди, Франция. Его родителей звали Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth и Hawise. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Idonea FitzWilliam. Он умер перед 1180 г. в местности честир, чешир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Helto Marmion de Bois d'Elle nació en Fontenay-le-Marmion, Calvados, Normandie, Francia como hijo de Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth y Hawise. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Idonea FitzWilliam. Murió antes de 1180, en Chester, Cheshire, Inglaterra.
